summary: This topic addresses the charges associated with using the integrated payment gateway (Razorpay) for online transactions, including potential GST and other bank charges, and highlights the need to clarify these rates and decide on their handling.
steps: As an administrator or configurator, obtain detailed information regarding Razorpay's charges, including the base rate, applicable GST, and any other bank charges.
Decide whether the institution will absorb these payment gateway charges or pass them on to the student.
Optionally, explore and compare alternative payment gateways if the current charges are deemed uncompetitive.
business_rules: Payment gateway charges are levied by the service provider (Razorpay) for facilitating online transactions.
These charges may include GST as per government regulations.
important_notes: Payment gateway charges are visible during online payment tests (e.g., during new admission registration).
The exact rate and a detailed breakdown of these charges must be confirmed directly with Razorpay.
The institution needs to make a clear policy decision on whether to absorb these charges or have the student bear them.
The possibility of integrating the institution's own bank gateway or exploring other competitive options exists.
questions: What are the charges associated with using the online payment gateway (Razorpay)?
Do the payment gateway charges include GST or other bank fees?
Who is responsible for bearing the payment gateway charges – the institution or the student?
Can we integrate our own bank's payment gateway or explore other options?
related_topics: New Admission Registration Form (Online Payment & Email Notifications)
